title: QUIC RFC 9000 MAX_DATA and MAX_STREAM_DATA Runtime Credit Publication Work Item
summary: Add direct protected-packet proof and permanent benchmark coverage for active-runtime MAX_DATA and MAX_STREAM_DATA credit publication after receive-side application reads consume bytes.
planned_changes:
- Multi-tag the existing protected-packet read proof so the receiver credit-advertisement requirements point at real outbound MAX_DATA and MAX_STREAM_DATA packets.
- Add negative runtime coverage proving empty reads do not publish MAX_DATA or MAX_STREAM_DATA frames.
- Keep the existing terminal-stream MAX_STREAM_DATA suppression proof linked to the bounded runtime credit publication slice.
- Add a permanent BenchmarkDotNet case for the stream-state helper branch that performs an empty read without publishing credit.
- Add focused architecture and verification artifacts for the bounded runtime credit publication slice.
verification_plan: Run the focused RFC 9000 S4P1/S4P2/S13P3 requirement-home tests, run the MAX_DATA and MAX_STREAM_DATA frame-codec fuzz coverage, run the `QuicConnectionStreamStateBenchmarks` BenchmarkDotNet suite with the Dry job, render/check the touched SpecTrace artifacts, and preserve evidence under `artifacts/verification/20260425-rfc9000-max-credit-runtime/`.
out_of_scope: Packet coalescing or opportunistic batching policy.
Changing DATA_BLOCKED, STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED, or STREAMS_BLOCKED behavior.
completion_notes: Completed as a proof and benchmark topoff over the existing runtime MAX_DATA and MAX_STREAM_DATA publication path; no production behavior changed. A 2026-05-01 S4P2 blocked-signal topoff added direct negative and edge requirement-home proof that blocked signals do not create credit without application consumption and do not alter the credit values computed after bytes are consumed.
